title: The Thirsty Crow
lines: A Crow, in great need of water, found a pitcher that seemed to contain water.
He looked in, but the water was so far down that his beak could not reach it.
He tried every way to knock the pitcher over, but it was too strong.
Then he thought of another way.
Picking up some stones and bits of gravel, he dropped them into the pitcher one by one.
Each bit of stone raised the water a little, until at last it rose near the top.
Then the Crow could drink and quench his thirst.
moral: Patience and perseverance overcome great obstacles.
